title: platform-user — retention windows
description: Two-phase retention. Phase 1 (soft-delete, 90-day hold) starts on offboard — the row is soft-deleted, access is revoked, but the display_name + department + dates remain resolvable so audit rows written before offboard still name the staffer clearly. Phase 2 (hard delete) fires after the hold expires — the row is purged, audit rows referencing this PlatformUser are RETAINED with an anonymised actor placeholder (compliance retention prevails over erasure). Audit correlation IDs are preserved indefinitely.
compliance_regimes_referenced: soc2 CC8.1 (7y change-record retention on the audit trail)
gdpr Art. 17 (bounded erasure — the audit-retention overrides Art. 17 for the employment record)
hr_compliance regional employment-record retention floors (typically 3–7 years, satisfied by audit-trail retention)

purge.trigger: N/A — active rows are never purged. Only offboarded rows enter the retention hold.
PlatformUserOffboarded event fires → status transitions to 'offboarded' → deleted_at set to offboarded_at → hold clock starts
purge.action: soft delete (row survives with deleted_at set)
purge.job: N/A — soft-delete happens inline with the offboard action

notes: During the hold, the PlatformUser row remains queryable via a `platform.platform_users.view.offboarded` scope on the index endpoint. The row is EXCLUDED from the default list.

Manual restoration during the hold is supported via a super_admin's `platform-user:restore-from-offboarding` command — requires a compliance-officer co-signer. Restoration reverses the deleted_at + resets status to 'suspended' (never straight to 'active'; requires a separate `restore` action).

purge.trigger: deleted_at + 90 days < now() AND status = 'offboarded' AND RevokePlatformUserAccessJob completed successfully
purge.action: hard delete + cascade PlatformProfile hard delete in same transaction

notes: The Identity row is NOT purged — it may still be referenced by a User row (the same human as a tenant customer) OR live on unattached for a subsequent hire.
Audit rows keyed by this PlatformUser as actor are RETAINED with `user_id` replaced by an anonymised placeholder (`[REDACTED-PLATFORM-USER]`). The `user_type` is preserved for filter purposes.

The `platform-user:audit-report` command run BEFORE hard delete generates a compliance-filing PDF that HR retains for the jurisdictional employment-record retention floor (usually 3–7 years). Post-hard-delete queries against the audit trail resolve the actor to the redacted placeholder — the PDF is the enduring record.

hot.days: while linked PlatformUser is active OR in retention hold

purge.trigger: PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob transaction — same commit as PlatformUser hard delete
purge.action: hard delete via foreign-key cascade (RESTRICT on the PlatformUser side means the transaction must be explicit)

cold.description: 7 years total. Rotates to S3 Glacier via audit::RotateAuditColdStorageJob at 365d. Owned by the audit module; this file references the policy.

purge.trigger: audit module's own retention policy — platform-user does not purge audit rows directly
purge.action: anonymise-not-delete via audit module lifecycle. PlatformUser hard delete triggers a user_id anonymisation on the audit rows (see phase 2 notes) but the row bodies survive.

hot.days: while linked PlatformUser is active OR in retention hold

purge.trigger: PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob detects zero active sessions AND all historical sessions have ended_at set. Cascade behaviour is RESTRICT on the PlatformUser FK.
purge.action: no purge — historical impersonation sessions survive with the actor anonymised (owned by access/delegation module)

audit_correlation_preserved_indefinitely.description: Correlation IDs written by the `platform.audit` middleware are ALWAYS preserved. Even after PlatformUser hard delete, cross-referencing audit rows via correlation_id remains possible. This is the load-bearing evidence for SOC 2 CC8.1 — an auditor can trace 'the sequence of platform-admin actions that led to this tenant-plane change' 5 years after the acting staffer left.

grace_beyond_hold_days.description: PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob checks that RevokePlatformUserAccessJob completed successfully BEFORE hard-deleting. If access revocation is stuck (extremely rare — typically a bug), the retention hold effectively extends indefinitely + emits `platform_user.retention.blocked` metric with `reason=revocation_incomplete`. Ops must intervene manually.
